title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Repository Trace: Version Policy
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git version policy increment in a signed-off release evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Version Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — Release v5.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

steps.title: Bound Version Policy

theory case — Release v6.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. content.text: Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Version Policy while producing a signed-off release evidence record.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Release v5.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. Competency probe: Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity. A defensible solution applies Version Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a signed-off release evidence record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-version-policy — Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
git status --porcelain=v1
git show --no-patch --format=fuller HEAD
git tag -a "v2.1.0" -m 'Verified release candidate' HEAD
git show --no-patch "v2.1.0^{commit}"
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Version Policy evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-version-policy-9oqx7f" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Version Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
